steps.title: Track Rafael's goals
steps.content: The passage explains that Rafael had heard about the Ureña girls from his brother and wanted to meet them. It also says he hoped to make the acquaintance of their father, the poet Nicolás Ureña, at Mamá's house.

steps.title: Reject unsupported motives
steps.content: A invents a desire for formal instruction and mentorship. B wrongly transfers the narrator's learning situation to Rafael. C invents a plan to recite poems and request comments. The stated reason is that Rafael hoped to meet both the sisters and their father. The answer is D.
